text.title: LoRA and QLoRA — Low-Rank Adaptation Core Rules

rule.statement: LoRA-only training MUST use bf16 on Ampere+ GPUs (A100/A6000/3090/4090/5090).
rule.rationale: bf16 has wider dynamic range than fp16; far fewer NaN crashes.

rule.statement: QLoRA base quantisation MUST be nf4 (not int4 / int8).
rule.rationale: nf4 is the only 4-bit format that preserves quality for LLMs as of 2026.

rule.statement: QLoRA compute_dtype MUST be bf16 (not fp16) when GPU supports it.
rule.rationale: bf16 compute on nf4 weights is the empirically stable combination.

rule.statement: LoRA alpha MUST be 2× rank by default; deviate only with measured evidence.
Gradient checkpointing MUST be enabled when VRAM headroom is < 4GB.
5-step smoke test (load, fwd, bwd, opt step, save) MUST pass before launching a multi-hour run.
rule.rationale: Dtype / device errors are common; smoke catches them in <2 min.
